description: Emit `dms.CollectionItemIdSequence` unconditionally in the core DDL phase for PostgreSQL and SQL Server, add any shared constants needed for the new core object, and preserve deterministic ordering so the sequence is created before any table definitions that can reference it. Acceptance: both dialects create the sequence idempotently, existing `ChangeVersionSequence` behavior is unchanged, and unit tests explicitly assert presence and ordering.
steps-to-verify: Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj
Inspect the updated core DDL assertions or snapshots to confirm `CollectionItemIdSequence` is emitted before any collection table defaults.

description: Teach relational table emission to use stable-table identity metadata when rendering columns so `CollectionItemId` gets the correct engine-specific default sourced from `dms.CollectionItemIdSequence` on `DbTableKind.Collection` and `DbTableKind.ExtensionCollection` tables only. Acceptance: PostgreSQL emits `nextval(...)`, SQL Server emits `NEXT VALUE FOR ...`, `BaseCollectionItemId` and `ParentCollectionItemId` remain plain locator columns, and root tables plus collection-aligned extension-scope tables do not gain unintended defaults.
steps-to-verify: Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj
Inspect focused relational-emitter assertions to confirm defaults appear only on `CollectionItemId` columns for collection and extension-child tables.

description: Add or refresh regression coverage that locks DDL emission to the permissive shared relational-model pipeline and prevents this story from reintroducing global semantic-identity fail-fast. Acceptance: DDL continues to build from `RelationalModelSetPasses.CreateDefault()`, generic fixtures without semantic identity still emit DDL, and fixtures with compiled semantic identity prove DDL consumes the metadata when it exists.
steps-to-verify: Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj
Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Add a dedicated DDL golden fixture based on the focused stable-key sample scenario already used by relational-model tests. Acceptance: committed PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL plus relational-model manifests cover root-level collections, nested collections, collection-aligned extension scopes, root-level `_ext` child collections, nested extension child collections, and collection-aligned `_ext` child collections; the fixture proves `CollectionItemId` defaults, PKs on stable identity, sibling-order uniques, semantic-identity uniques when present, conditional `(CollectionItemId, <Root>_DocumentId)` target uniques only where needed, and immediate-parent cascade FKs without redundant root-cascade edges.
steps-to-verify: If snapshots changed intentionally, run: UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj
Run again without blessing: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj

description: Update FK-supporting index emission and deterministic ordering rules for the stable-identity constraint inventory introduced by this story. Acceptance: new composite parent/root and extension-parent FKs receive the expected supporting indexes without duplicates, canonical SQL ordering remains stable across both dialects, and focused regression coverage proves constraint/index ordering does not drift when stable-identity metadata is present.
steps-to-verify: Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj
Inspect updated focused fixture and regression snapshots to confirm new FK-support indexes and stable statement ordering.

description: Add a new authoritative fixture that combines DS 5.2 core input with the Sample extension input, rather than Sample alone. Acceptance: the new fixture directory has a `fixture.json` that lists both schema inputs, expected PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL plus DDL/effective-schema/relational-model manifests are committed, and the authoritative outputs show stable collection row identity on real extension attachment scenarios against actual Ed-Fi base resources.
steps-to-verify: If snapshots changed intentionally, run: UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "Category=Authoritative"

Run again without blessing: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "Category=Authoritative"

description: Add or extend PostgreSQL generated-DDL apply test helpers so a test can provision a fresh database from the shared full emitter, apply the same generated DDL a second time, and run targeted schema/data assertions safely. Acceptance: the harness can be reused by stable-key smoke tests, supports second-apply idempotency checks, and exposes enough helpers to query sequence existence, column defaults, FK metadata, and representative inserted rows.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the PostgreSQL integration test database is configured and reachable.
Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj

description: Add narrow PostgreSQL generated-DDL apply smoke tests for the focused stable-key fixture. Acceptance: tests generate full DDL via the shared emitter, provision a fresh database, apply the same DDL a second time successfully, assert `dms.CollectionItemIdSequence` exists, confirm representative collection and extension-child tables allocate `CollectionItemId` via defaults, assert targeted FK metadata for the immediate-parent chain and the absence of redundant root-cascade paths, prove representative nested and extension-child inserts satisfy the emitted FK shapes, and verify delete cascades follow the immediate-parent chain.
steps-to-verify: Ensure the PostgreSQL integration test database is configured and reachable.
Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj

description: This is the last task. Refresh regression goldens and manifests affected as fallout from stable collection row identity, then review the branch diff from main for deterministic-artifact churn before closing the story. Acceptance: small regression fixtures such as `small/nested` and `small/ext` are updated only where the new sequence/default or constraint ordering changes require it, authoritative fixture churn is limited to stable-identity-related DDL changes, and the final diff review calls out anything unexpected before merge. If there is an issue, write out a PROBLEMS.md file
steps-to-verify: Run: dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj
